task_completion.description: Generate a 3D surface of the skull (bone mask)
task_completion.visual_evidence: The InVesalius 3 interface shows a 3D rendering of a skull (bone mask) in the 3D viewer pane.

task_completion.description: Access the animation or movie export tool
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent opens the export movie, animation, or sequence dialog/panel within InVesalius 3.
The animation settings show rotation parameters being configured, such as setting the number of frames to at least 12 or selecting a video format.

task_completion.description: Export the animation to the specified directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog or progress bar indicates the files are being saved to either /home/ga/Documents/skull_rotation_frames/ (for PNGs) or /home/ga/Documents/skull_rotation.avi (for video).

task_completion.description: Verify the exported animation files exist
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal window shows either the skull_rotation.avi file or a series of at least 12 PNG files in the correct target directory.

integrity.description: Agent used the InVesalius 3 GUI to generate the outputs
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with InVesalius 3 menus, buttons, and dialogs rather than terminal commands or scripts generating the 3D model and animation.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual CT Cranium dataset
integrity.visual_evidence: The InVesalius 3 application displays a genuine human skull/cranium in the 2D slices and 3D viewer, not a dummy or empty dataset.

integrity.description: Results appear to come from genuine software interaction
integrity.visual_evidence: The exported files are seen being saved directly from the InVesalius application (e.g., via a save dialog), rather than being downloaded, copied from a pre-existing source, or created via a text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target directory for frames is exactly '/home/ga/Documents/skull_rotation_frames' and for video is '/home/ga/Documents/skull_rotation.avi'. If exporting frames, there must be at least 12 frames. If exporting video, the file size must be at least 102401 bytes. The 180-degree rotation requirement is not strictly validated by code but should be visually apparent in the settings or output.
